role_title: GTM Growth Product Manager, Agentic Systems

location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (GTM systems / AI workflow focus)

level_signals: Comp range $239K–$265K base consistent with Senior/Staff IC at OpenAI
Owns roadmap for a portfolio of GTM systems and AI-powered tools

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 8+ years of product management experience across agentic systems, GTM systems, enterprise SaaS, revenue technology, or AI workflow products
Have strong fluency in modern GTM systems such as Salesforce, marketing automation, lead-to-quote, enrichment, routing, attribution, billing, or customer lifecycle tools
Are strong at cross-functional influence, driving strategic alignment, and executive communication.
Are highly analytical and comfortable defining KPIs, running experiments, and using data to prioritize product investments
Thrive in ambiguous, fast-moving environments where the first version of the strategy needs to be shaped, tested, and improved quickly

scope.surface: GTM AI systems portfolio spanning lead capture, prioritization, routing, attribution, sales productivity, data movement, and revenue workflows
core_responsibilities: Deeply understand problems faced by GTM teams and customers, and identify opportunities where AI systems can make buying and adopting our technology faster and more intuitive.
Define and drive the product vision and roadmap for a portfolio of GTM AI systems
Build AI-powered products that improve pipeline generation, seller productivity, account prioritization, routing, attribution, and GTM execution
Translate GTM strategy and operational needs into scalable product capabilities, PRDs, success metrics, and engineering priorities
Lead cross-functional execution across engineering, vendors, systems owners, and business stakeholders
Improve the data foundations behind GTM systems, including entity resolution, enrichment, scoring, and data movement.
Use experimentation, metrics, and customer feedback to measure impact on pipeline quality, conversion, sales efficiency, and revenue execution
Drive adoption and change management for new GTM capabilities across distributed teams

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area (not vague)
High-impact role with direct exposure to GTM leadership and executive stakeholders
OpenAI brand and mission provide strong career signal
red_flags: No equity or bonus details mentioned despite seniority level
Hybrid requirement (3 days/week in San Francisco) eliminates fully remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
No nice-to-haves listed, making bar-setting entirely on the must-haves — less flexible on candidate fit

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and GTM context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed explicitly as KPIs)
ambiguities: The JD lists requirements under 'You might thrive in this role if you:' — language could imply preferred rather than required, but all items are unconditional bullets describing the expected candidate profile. Treated as hard must-haves given seniority and specificity.
No nice-to-haves section exists in the JD; all requirements collapsed into must-haves. This may under-represent flexibility in hiring.
